en: If your company works in the State of California, does your company have an Injury, Illness, Prevention Program (IIPP)? \nCal/OSHA Injury & Illness Prevention Program
Does your program satisfy your responsibility under the law for ensuring your employees follow the safety rules of the facility?
Does your program satisfy your responsibility under the law for advising the owner/operator of any unique hazards presented by the contractor's work and any hazards found by the contractor?
Does your company have a documented H2S Awareness Specific program?
Does your company have a documented HAZCOM program?
Does your company have a documented Hazardous Waste Operations and Emergency Response program?
Does your company have a documented NORM (Naturally Occurring Radioactive Material) program?
Does your company have a documented PSM (Process Safety Management) program?
When applicable, does your company require eye protection (ANSI-Z87.1)(29 CFR 1910.133 & 1926.102)?
When applicable, does your company require hand protection (29 CFR 1910.138)?
When applicable, does your company require hard hats (ANSI-Z89.1)(29 CFR 1910.135 & 1926.100)?
When applicable, does your company require safety shoes (ANSI-Z41/ASTM F-2412/F2413)(29 CFR 1910.136 & 1926.96)?
Does your company's Electrical program include NFPA 70E? \nNFPA 70E
Avetta PQF - United States HSE Programs

Does your company have a program that addresses procedures for calling the appropriate one-call center by dialing 811 at least 48 hours before digging, driving equipment into the ground or engaging in any earth-moving activities?
Does your company ensure the utilities are properly marked prior to digging through local utility companies?
If no, please provide a detailed explanation as to why Call Before you Dig is not addressed.
